stemHtml: An aircraft is executing an approach followed by a visual manoeuvre (circling) in order to land on a different runway. When should the pilot descent below the MDA/H?

options.a: Level off at MDA/H, continue the downwind leg at MDA/H and commence descent when turning base to intercept the extended centreline of the landing runway.
options.b: Descent below the MDA/H as soon as visual reference with the aerodrome is established.
options.c: Descent below the MDA/H when visual reference is established and can be maintained, the landing threshold is in sight, and the aircraft is in a position to carry out a landing with required obstacle clearance assured.
options.d: Descent below the MDA/H when the aircraft reaches a position from which a normal descent to the runway can be geometrically planned.
